_meta.description: Multi-finding correlation rules. Each rule fires when findings matching `primary` and `secondary` co-exist in the same scan. Severity narrative is upgraded to reflect the chain, not the individual links.

schema.name: Chain name
schema.primary_cwe: CWE pattern that anchors the chain
schema.secondary_cwe: CWE pattern that enables the chain (pipe-separated alternatives)
schema.same_file_required: If true, both findings must be in the same file
schema.narrative: How to describe the chain in a report
schema.chained_severity: critical | high — what the combined chain rates

rules.name: IDOR + Missing Auth = Pre-Auth Account Takeover
rules.narrative: IDOR on {primary.file}:{primary.line} accepts arbitrary user IDs, and {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} ships without an auth check. Chained, this means an unauthenticated attacker can read or modify any user's data without ever logging in. Either finding alone is bad; together they are a pre-auth account-takeover path.

rules.name: SSRF + Cloud-Metadata Leak = IAM Compromise
rules.narrative: SSRF on {primary.file}:{primary.line} can hit `169.254.169.254`, and the response leak at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} echoes upstream body back to the caller. The attacker reads IAM temporary credentials directly. The combined finding is full-account cloud takeover, not just internal-network probing.

rules.name: Path Traversal + Arbitrary Write = RCE via cron/init
rules.narrative: Path traversal on {primary.file}:{primary.line} combined with arbitrary write at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} lets the attacker drop a webshell into a routable directory, overwrite cron files in /etc/cron.d/, or replace systemd unit files. Reproducible RCE — not just file read.

rules.name: Stored XSS + No CSP = Full Session Hijack
rules.narrative: Stored XSS at {primary.file}:{primary.line} without a CSP header (gap at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line}) means there is no in-browser mitigation. Any user who loads the affected page is fully compromised — token exfil, action-on-behalf-of, persistent re-injection.

rules.name: Prompt Injection + Tool Access = Data Exfiltration
rules.narrative: Prompt-injection vector at {primary.file}:{primary.line} combined with the unrestricted tool surface at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} allows an attacker to instruct the agent to call `fetch_url` / `execute_code` / `read_file` against attacker-controlled targets. The user's data and the agent's secrets exfil through tools the user never invoked.

rules.name: Mass Assignment + Missing Auth = Privilege Escalation
rules.narrative: Mass assignment at {primary.file}:{primary.line} accepts `{role: 'admin'}`. The endpoint at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} doesn't gate by role. Any user can self-promote to admin in one POST.

rules.name: Open Redirect + OAuth Callback = Code Theft
rules.narrative: Open redirect on {primary.file}:{primary.line} with OAuth/SAML auth flow at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} permits stealing the authorization code or SAML response by redirecting the victim post-auth to an attacker domain. Token-grade compromise, not just phishing.

rules.name: SQLi + Hardcoded DB Admin Creds = Lateral Movement
rules.narrative: SQL injection at {primary.file}:{primary.line} can pivot to other databases when the connection at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} uses an over-privileged hardcoded account that's reused across services. The blast radius is multi-tenant, not just this one DB.

rules.name: Reflected XSS + CSRF Token in DOM = Stateful CSRF
rules.narrative: Reflected XSS at {primary.file}:{primary.line} can read the CSRF token from the DOM (the protection at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} relies on token validity, not origin). XSS forges a valid CSRF request, bypassing CSRF protection entirely.

rules.name: JWT alg:none + Hardcoded Secret = Forge Any Token
rules.narrative: The same file accepts `alg:none` JWTs ({primary.line}) AND stores a verifier secret ({secondary.line}). Forge a valid token for any user (sub of choice) — full impersonation without a credential phish.

rules.name: Webhook Without Signature + Replay Window = Action Replay
rules.narrative: Webhook handler at {primary.file}:{primary.line} doesn't verify signatures, and the action handler at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line} has no idempotency / replay window. An attacker submits forged or replayed webhook events to drive state changes.

rules.name: User Input in System Prompt + No Output Validation = Prompt-to-SQL
rules.narrative: User input flows into the system prompt at {primary.file}:{primary.line}, and the LLM output is later used as a SQL fragment at {secondary.file}:{secondary.line}. The attacker steers the LLM into emitting malicious SQL the app then executes. Prompt-driven SQLi.
